name: Kesäkeitto (Summer Vegetable Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb String beans, fresh, cut into 1/4" strips
1/4 lb Spinach, fresh, washed and finely chopped
1/2 lb Shrimp, medium-sized, cooked and cleaned (optional)
2 tbsp Parsley or dill, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
Select the youngest, freshest vegetables you can find. Wash, scrape, or cut them to the sizes specified in the ingredient list. Then, except for the spinach, place all of the vegetables in a 2-3 quart pot, cover with cold water and add the salt. Bring to a boil uncovered and simmer for 5 minutes or until the vegetables are tender. Add spinach and cook another 5 minutes. Remove from heat and strain the liquid through a fine sieve into a bowl. Set the vegetable stock and the vegetables aside in separate bowls.
Melt 2 tablespoons of butter in the pan over moderate heat. Remove from heat and stir in the flour. Slowly pour in the hot vegetable stock, beating vigorously with a wire whisk. Add the milk and continue to beat until smooth. In a small bowl, combine the egg yolk and cream. Whisk in 1 cup of the hot soup, 2 tablespoons at a time. Reverse the process and slowly whisk the warmed egg yolk and cream mixture back into the soup. Add the reserved vegetables to the soup and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat, add cooked shrimp if using, and simmer uncovered for 3-5 minutes or until heated through.
Taste and season with white pepper as well as additional salt if necessary. Serve in a soup tureen and garnish with finely chopped parsley or dill.
